steps.title: Understand the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence negates one fact (“Chaucer didn’t write in Modern English”) and then states the true alternative (“he wrote in what we now call Middle English”). The blank must signal a correction or substitution — not a sequence, cause-and-effect, or comparison.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "rather,": signals correction or substitution (“not X, rather Y”), matching the shift from the false claim to the true alternative. ✓
  • "finally,": signals the last item in a sequence, but no sequence exists in this sentence. ✗
  • "as a result,": signals cause-and-effect, but not writing in Modern English did not cause Chaucer to write in Middle English. ✗
  • "similarly,": signals likeness or agreement, but the two clauses contrast rather than agree. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
